name: Rick Heidner
bio: Rick Heidner is a self-made businessman who built Illinois Gaming Investors, a video gaming terminal company, from a $10,000 loan into a multi-state operation. He has no prior political office experience. Heidner is running as an outsider candidate who argues 40 years of private-sector budgeting qualifies him to fix Illinois's fiscal problems. His self-funded campaign emphasizes an 'Illinoisans First' message targeting population flight, property taxes, and the SAFE-T Act.

jacket.note: Self-funded businessman. Video gaming industry background raises potential conflict-of-interest questions. Running hard on SAFE-T Act rollback and property tax relief.

red_flags.label: Video Gaming Industry Background
red_flags.detail: Heidner built his fortune in the video gaming terminal industry. As governor, he would oversee the state agency regulating that same industry — a potential conflict of interest that opponents and watchdog groups may scrutinize.

red_flags.label: No Prior Elected or Public-Sector Experience
red_flags.detail: Heidner has never held public office or managed a government agency. His entire career is in private business.

policy_positions: Property tax relief — middle-class families pay $4,472/year above national average
Term limits for governor — two terms, then go home
Rollback SAFE-T Act — restore judge authority to detain dangerous defendants
Lower corporate taxes and reduce red tape for small businesses
School vouchers — parental choice if school is failing
Electronic monitoring reform for pretrial defendants
